question_text: Let 𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛 , 𝑛≥2, be a random sample from a 𝑁(−𝜃, 𝜃) distribution, where 𝜃> 0 is an unknown parameter. Then which one of the following options is

options.A: 𝑖=1 is a minimal sufficient statistic
options.B: 𝑖=1 2 is a minimal sufficient statistic
options.D: 𝑖=1 is a uniformly minimum variance unbiased estimator of 𝜃
